description_long: This is a two-story suburban house with a large, steeply pitched hip roof and a covered front porch that spans the width of the building. The front facade features two prominent dormer windows above the porch, and the entryway is flanked by columns and railings. The house is surrounded by a small patch of grass and has a paved driveway leading to the front steps, with three cars parked in front. The windows are symmetrically arranged, with multiple rectangular panes on each side of the house. The exterior walls appear to be finished in a light-colored material, likely painted siding or stucco, and the roof is clad in gray shingles. The overall design suggests a blend of Colonial Revival and Craftsman influences, with a focus on symmetry and a welcoming porch.
description: A two-story house with a large hip roof, covered front porch, dormer windows, and a symmetrical facade. The exterior is light-colored with gray roofing and a grassy yard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, two dormer windows, main entryway, and three cars parked on the driveway.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the hip roof, symmetrical window placement, and the depth of the porch; cars are visible along the side.
description_view_2: Rear view with a simpler facade, fewer windows, and the back porch area visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the hip roof, porch wrapping around the side, and cars parked adjacent to the house.
attribute_entryway_design: Central front door with steps, covered porch, columns, and decorative railings
